input.initialPrompt:
I have a clean macOS machine and want to start developing .NET MAUI apps
for iOS and Android. I can't run commands right now, but I need a complete
setup plan. Tell me exactly what I need to install, how to discover the
right versions, and how to verify everything works. Please provide the
official recommended installation sources.

data.content:
Here's a complete .NET MAUI setup plan for macOS:

---

## 1. Install Xcode (iOS requirement)

- **Source:** Mac App Store or [developer.apple.com/xcode](https://developer.apple.com/xcode/)
- After install, run:
  ```
  xcode-select --install
  sudo xcodebuild -license accept
  ```
- **Version discovery:** MAUI docs list the minimum Xcode version per .NET release at [learn.microsoft.com/dotnet/maui](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/dotnet/maui/). Match your .NET version to the required Xcode version.

## 2. Install .NET SDK

- **Source:** [dot.net/download](https://dot.net/download) — get the latest LTS or STS release (currently .NET 8 LTS or .NET 9).
- Use the `.pkg` installer for macOS (arm64 for Apple Silicon, x64 for Intel).
- **Verify:**
  ```
  dotnet --version
  dotnet --list-sdks
  ```

## 3. Install the MAUI workload

```
dotnet workload install maui
```
- **Verify installed workloads:**
  ```
  dotnet workload list
  ```
  You should see `maui` (or `maui-ios`, `maui-android`, etc.).

## 4. Install JDK (Android requirement)

- The MAUI workload typically bundles OpenJDK via the Android workload. To confirm or install manually:
  - **Source:** [Microsoft OpenJDK](https://learn.microsoft.com/java/openjdk/download) (recommended) or Adoptium at [adoptium.net](https://adoptium.net/).
  - MAUI currently requires **JDK 17**.
- **Verify:**
  ```
  java -version
  ```

## 5. Android SDK & Emulator

- The `maui-android` workload auto-provisions the Android SDK. To manage it explicitly:
  - **Source:** [Android Studio](https://developer.android.com/studio) includes SDK Manager, or use the standalone command-line tools.
  - Accept licenses:
    ```
    dotnet build -t:InstallAndroidDependencies \
      -f net8.0-android \
      "-p:AndroidSdkDirectory=$HOME/Library/Developer/Xamarin/android-sdk-macosx" \
      -p:AcceptAndroidSDKLicenses=true
    ```
- Alternatively, open Android Studio → SDK Manager to install specific API levels and emulator images.

## 6. iOS Simulators

```
xcodebuild -downloadPlatform iOS
```
Or open **Xcode → Settings → Platforms** to download simulator runtimes.

## 7. Verify the full toolchain

```bash
# Create and run a test app on each target
dotnet new maui -n MauiTest
cd MauiTest

# Build for Android
dotnet build -f net8.0-android

# Build for iOS (simulator)
dotnet build -f net8.0-ios

# Run on iOS simulator
dotnet build -f net8.0-ios -t:Run \
  -p:_DeviceName=:v2:udid=$(xcrun simctl list devices available iPhone -j \
  | python3 -c "import sys,json; print(json.load(sys.stdin)['devices'][sorted([k for k in json.load(open('/dev/stdin') if False else sys.stdin)['devices'] if 'iOS' in k])[-1]][0]['udid'])" 2>/dev/null)
```
Or more simply, use an IDE (see below).

## 8. Recommended IDE

| Option | Source |
|---|---|
| **Visual Studio Code** + C# Dev Kit + .NET MAUI extension | [code.visualstudio.com](https://code.visualstudio.com/) |
| **JetBrains Rider** (full MAUI support) | [jetbrains.com/rider](https://www.jetbrains.com/rider/) |

> *Visual Studio for Mac has been retired.* VS Code with the MAUI extension is now Microsoft's recommended path on macOS.

## 9. Quick version-compatibility reference

Check this page before installing anything — it lists **exact** Xcode, JDK, Android SDK, and .NET SDK version requirements per MAUI release:

📄 **[.NET MAUI Supported Platforms](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/dotnet/maui/supported-platforms)**

---

**TL;DR install order:** Xcode → .NET SDK → `dotnet workload install maui` → verify with `dotnet new maui` → build for both targets. The workload handles most Android dependencies automatically.
